goal: Decode per-id status-effect semantics: where +0x71a (status id) is READ each frame and what behavior each id triggers.
headline: The +0x71a status-id field is NOT a per-id behavior selector. Across the ENTIRE corpus it is read only 8 times, in exactly two roles: (1) an index into the +0x5a0 immunity BITMASK to gate whether a hit's damage/effect applies, and (2) a bone/attach-point index handed to zz_0066408_ to position the hit-spark visual at the right joint. There is NO switch statement, NO status-descriptor table, and NO per-frame behavior dispatch keyed on +0x71a anywhere in the export. The wiki status behaviors (poison DoT, freeze, time-stop, aim-scramble, position-swap, transform, enlarge/shrink, barrier) are therefore NOT driven by this id in a readable engine table -- they are per-borg/per-move authored data plus dedicated (separately-named) handlers, and remain trace-only (T8) for the id->wiki-name mapping. The +0x71c field is an id-AGNOSTIC hitstun/freeze TIMER, not per-id.

statuses.behavior: DERIVED: The status id is used ONLY as a bit-index into the actor's immunity bitmask at +0x5a0. On a hit, the id is written to +0x71a masked to 6 bits (& 0x3f). Both the APPLY site and the damage formula gate on `(+0x5a0 & (1 << +0x71a))`: if the corresponding immunity bit is set, the actor is IMMUNE and the hit's damage/effect is skipped. There is NO code that reads +0x71a and branches to poison/freeze/timestop/etc. behavior -- the id does not select a behavior, it selects an immunity bit.
fields_touched: +0x5e0 (status flag word, checked & 0x20000000 to suppress effect)
statuses.tickFn: none -- +0x71a is not ticked or scanned each frame; it is written on hit and read only at hit-resolution and at render-attach time
statuses.immunityGateFn: 0x8003cd5c zz_003cd5c_ (chunk_0004.c:6693-6699) -- damage formula step 1: dmg=0 if (victim+0x5a0 & 1<<victim[0x71a])

statuses.behavior: DERIVED: In four collision-response sites the id is copied into a local and passed to zz_0066408_ as param_2, which uses it as an INDEX into a per-actor 0x30-byte transform/joint array (`param_1 + param_2 * 0x30 + 0x8d4`) to fetch a bone position, then applies zz_004516c_ to place a hit-spark/impact effect at that joint. `if (param_2 < 0)` falls back to a default bone from +0x4b0->+0x10, confirming this is a BONE/ATTACH-POINT index. This is purely cosmetic effect placement -- not gameplay behavior.
statuses.evidence: *(undefined1 *)(param_1 + 0x188) = *(undefined1 *)(iVar3 + 0x71a); zz_0066408_(iVar3,(int)*(char *)(param_1 + 0x188),(float *)(param_1 + 0x20),(float *)(param_1 + 0x168));  /// consumer: if (param_2 < 0) { param_2 = (int)*(char *)(*(int *)(param_1 + 0x4b0) + 0x10); } zz_004516c_((float *)(param_1 + param_2 * 0x30 + 0x8d4),param_3,param_4);

timerField_0x71c.role: DERIVED: id-AGNOSTIC hitstun / freeze timer, NOT per-status-id.
timerField_0x71c.tickBehavior: Each frame: `if (+0x71c != 0) { +0x71c -= 1; if (still != 0) { +0x5f4 = FLOAT_804373dc; +0x1dc8 = same; return; } ... }`. While the timer is nonzero the actor's animation/physics rate (+0x5f4 / +0x1dc8) is FORCED to a fixed (frozen) value and the function early-returns, i.e. the actor is stun-locked; when it hits 0 the normal rate (+0x768-derived) is restored. Confirmed by ~15 movement/state handlers (chunks 11,15,16,18,20,22,26,28,30) all wrapping their normal-movement body in `if (+0x71c == 0) { ...normal movement... }`, so a nonzero +0x71c freezes movement uniformly regardless of id.
timerField_0x71c.applyBehavior: Set on hit to max(existing, moveData+4) at chunk_0003.c:7623-7624, :7638-7639, :8048-8049 -- a hitstun duration from the move's hit record, id-independent. Also hard-set to 6 in several throw/grab paths (chunk_0007.c:2942,2975,3010,3045,3080,3113).

immunityMask_0x5a0.role: u32 status-immunity bitmask on the actor; bit index == status id (+0x71a).

writers.meaning: immune to ALL statuses -- full invincibility window (spawn/wake-up i-frames)
clears immunity bit 13 (1<<13 = 0x2000) i.e. becomes vulnerable to status id 13 only, in that state

immunityMask_0x5a0.note: chunk_0067.c:3972-3973 `switch(*(iVar2 + 0x5a0))` is a FALSE POSITIVE for status: that iVar2 is a GX display object and DAT_cc008000 is the GameCube GX FIFO register (0xCC008000) -- it is graphics-pipeline code, not actor status. Not counted.

newFunctions.name: resolve_hitbox_target_effects_and_damage
newFunctions.role: STATUS APPLY: on a landed hit, writes status id to victim +0x71a (from moveData[+1], masked &0x3f), sets hitstun timer +0x71c = max(cur, moveData+4), and triggers the on-hit effect (FUN_800061a8) unless the victim is immune (+0x5a0 & 1<<id) or suppressed (+0x5e0 & 0x20000000). Already partially known; this confirms it as the sole +0x71a WRITER.

newFunctions.name: FUN_8005a378
newFunctions.role: HITSTUN/FREEZE TIMER TICK (per-frame): decrements +0x71c; while nonzero forces the actor's anim/physics rate fields (+0x5f4,+0x1dc8) to a frozen constant and early-returns (stun-lock); on reaching 0 restores the normal +0x768-derived rate. This is the per-frame 'status tick' the tracker was looking for -- but it is ID-AGNOSTIC (does not read +0x71a).
newFunctions.evidence: if (*(char *)(param_1 + 0x71c) != '\0') { cVar4 = *(char *)(param_1 + 0x71c) + -1; *(char *)(param_1 + 0x71c) = cVar4; ... if (cVar4 != '\0') { *(float *)(param_1 + 0x5f4) = FLOAT_804373dc; ... return; } }

newFunctions.name: zz_003cd5c_
newFunctions.role: DAMAGE FORMULA (already decoded as (ah) in behavior-notes): its step 1 is the status-immunity gate that READS +0x71a as a bit-index into victim +0x5a0 to zero the damage when immune. Confirmed as the second +0x71a reader.

newFunctions.name: zz_0066408_
newFunctions.role: BONE-INDEXED EFFECT PLACEMENT: takes a bone index (frequently the status id +0x71a) and applies a coordinate transform from the actor's 0x30-byte joint array (+0x8d4) to place an impact/spark effect. This is why +0x71a is read in the collision-response paths -- for VISUAL attach-point, not behavior. Negative index falls back to a default bone.

newFunctions.name: FUN_800061a8
newFunctions.role: ON-HIT EFFECT TRIGGER (thin wrapper -> zz_00061f0_ 0x800061f0): installs a timed effect descriptor from a PTR table (&PTR_DAT_802c3770)[type] into the actor's effect slot (+0x7ec/+0x7e4). Called by the APPLY fn when not immune. Selects effect by the hardcoded arg (1), NOT by +0x71a -- so the on-hit spark is fixed, not per-status-id.
newFunctions.evidence: *(undefined **)(iVar2 + 0x7ec) = (&PTR_DAT_802c3770)[param_2]; ... param_2 passed as literal 1 from FUN_800061a8

statusDescriptorTable.searched: Grepped all 80 chunks for every read/write of +0x71a (8 total occurrences), for switch(...+0x71a...), and for array-index patterns using +0x71a. No status-descriptor table indexed by the status id exists in the export. The only tables keyed near the status system are: the immunity BITMASK +0x5a0 (1 bit per id, boolean immune/not-immune, no behavior payload) and the per-joint transform array +0x8d4 (used for effect placement). Neither carries per-id behavior (no damage-mult, no DoT rate, no lock flags, no input-scramble).

portNote: status.ts (ATK-010 shell) CANNOT yet be enabled for any wiki-named status behavior from this static corpus, because the engine does NOT implement wiki statuses via a per-id table keyed on +0x71a. What IS fully DERIVED and portable RIGHT NOW: (1) the status-IMMUNITY system -- an actor with immunity bit `1<<id` set in +0x5a0 takes ZERO damage/effect from a hit carrying that id (chunk_0004.c:6693-6699); the wake-up/spawn i-frame windows set +0x5a0 = 0xffffffff (all-immune) and clear it to become vulnerable (chunk_0057). status.ts could implement `isImmune(actor, statusId) = (actor.immunityMask & (1<<statusId)) != 0` and the invincibility-window setter exactly. (2) The +0x71c hitstun/freeze TIMER -- decrement per frame, and while >0 lock movement/anim rate (FUN_8005a378); this is the mechanical basis for 'freeze' and 'time-stop' (both are just a nonzero stun timer on the victim) and IS portable as an id-agnostic stun timer. What is NOT derivable and stays trace-blocked (T8): the id->wiki-name mapping (which numeric id is 'poison' vs 'freeze' vs 'timestop') and the wiki behaviors themselves (poison DoT rate, mash-to-recover shortening, aim-scramble, position-swap, transform, enlarge/shrink) -- these live in per-borg/per-move authored data and dedicated handlers not reachable through +0x71a, consistent with behavior-notes' prior status-sweep 'no DoT/periodic drain via the status id' finding (Note 5 / (as)/(ax)).

resolvedCount.explanation: Zero status IDS map to a specific wiki behavior from static code, because per-id behavior dispatch does not exist in the export. However, the status FRAMEWORK is fully derived: 2 distinct id-USE roles (immunity-bit-index; bone-attach-index), 1 id-agnostic stun-timer tick, the immunity-mask writers/semantics, and 5 supporting functions (addresses above). The honest result is: the 'per-id semantics' the task hoped for are NOT in the corpus keyed on +0x71a -- the id is an immunity/attach selector, and wiki statuses are authored data, so T8 tracing remains required for the id->name catalog.
status_ts_enablable_now: id-agnostic hitstun/freeze timer (+0x71c decrement + movement lock)

summary: Traced every one of the 8 corpus reads/writes of the status id +0x71a plus the +0x71c timer. RESULT: +0x71a is NOT a per-id behavior selector -- it is (a) a bit-index into the +0x5a0 immunity bitmask (gates whether a hit's damage/effect lands; chunk_0004.c:6693-6699 damage-formula step 1, and chunk_0003.c:7648 apply-time trigger gate) and (b) a bone/attach-point index passed to zz_0066408_ to place the hit-spark visual (chunks 20/23/28/36). The APPLY function is 0x8002e2a8 resolve_hitbox_target_effects_and_damage (writes +0x71a &0x3f and +0x71c hitstun from move data). The per-frame TICK is 0x8005a378 FUN_8005a378, which decrements +0x71c and freezes movement while nonzero -- an id-AGNOSTIC hitstun/freeze timer (confirmed by ~15 handlers gating movement on `+0x71c == 0`). There is NO switch on +0x71a and NO status-descriptor table; the wiki status behaviors are authored per-borg/per-move data, not an engine table keyed on the id. So the T8 'per-id status semantics' blocker is REAL and stays trace-only for the id->name catalog. What CAN be ported to status.ts today: the immunity check, the invincibility-window set/clear, and the id-agnostic hitstun/freeze timer -- all fully DERIVED. Zero status ids resolved to a specific wiki behavior (poison/freeze/etc.) from static code.
